testcase.name: test_login_with_valid_credentials
testcase.failure: backend/tests/test_security_audit.py:139: in test_login_with_valid_credentials assert response.status_code == 200, f"Login failed with status {response.status_code}: {response.text}" E AssertionError: Login failed with status 429: {"error":"Rate limit exceeded: 5 per 1 minute"} E assert 429 == 200 E + where 429 = <Response [429]>.status_code

testcase.name: test_login_with_invalid_password
testcase.failure: backend/tests/test_security_audit.py:156: in test_login_with_invalid_password assert response.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {response.status_code}" E AssertionError: Expected 401, got 429 E assert 429 == 401 E + where 429 = <Response [429]>.status_code

testcase.name: test_login_with_nonexistent_user
testcase.failure: backend/tests/test_security_audit.py:166: in test_login_with_nonexistent_user assert response.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {response.status_code}" E AssertionError: Expected 401, got 429 E assert 429 == 401 E + where 429 = <Response [429]>.status_code

testcase.name: test_session_cookie_settings
testcase.failure: backend/tests/test_security_audit.py:177: in test_session_cookie_settings assert response.status_code == 200, f"Login failed: {response.text}" E AssertionError: Login failed: {"error":"Rate limit exceeded: 5 per 1 minute"} E assert 429 == 200 E + where 429 = <Response [429]>.status_code

testcase.name: test_file_upload_validation_exists
testcase.failure: backend/tests/test_security_audit.py:215: in test_file_upload_validation_exists assert login_response.status_code == 200, "Login failed" E AssertionError: Login failed E assert 429 == 200 E + where 429 = <Response [429]>.status_code

testcase.name: test_protected_endpoints_require_auth

failure.message: AssertionError: Endpoint /api/finance/accounts should require auth, got 404 assert 404 in [401, 403] + where 404 = <Response [404]>.status_code

testcase.failure: backend/tests/test_security_audit.py:256: in test_protected_endpoints_require_auth assert response.status_code in [401, 403], \ E AssertionError: Endpoint /api/finance/accounts should require auth, got 404 E assert 404 in [401, 403] E + where 404 = <Response [404]>.status_code

testcase.name: test_all_security_features_summary

failure.message: AssertionError: Some security features failed: {'security_headers': True, 'rate_limiting': True, 'authentication': False, 'cookie_security': False} assert False + where False = all(dict_values([True, True, False, False])) + where dict_values([True, True, False, False]) = <built-in method values of dict object at 0xfaba4e0c4200>() + where <built-in method values of dict object at 0xfaba4e0c4200> = {'authentication': False, 'cookie_security': False, 'rate_limiting': True, 'security_headers': True}.values

testcase.failure: backend/tests/test_security_audit.py:316: in test_all_security_features_summary assert all(results.values()), f"Some security features failed: {results}" E AssertionError: Some security features failed: {'security_headers': True, 'rate_limiting': True, 'authentication': False, 'cookie_security': False} E assert False E + where False = all(dict_values([True, True, False, False])) E + where dict_values([True, True, False, False]) = <built-in method values of dict object at 0xfaba4e0c4200>() E + where <built-in method values of dict object at 0xfaba4e0c4200> = {'authentication': False, 'cookie_security': False, 'rate_limiting': True, 'security_headers': True}.values
